jobs.name: Fireflies Meeting Processing & Jira Task Creation
jobs.description: Process all new Fireflies meeting transcripts, extract action items, create Jira tasks, and dispatch to agent team for execution

payload.message:
FIREFLIES PROCESSING PIPELINE

1. Query Fireflies MCP for all new meetings since last run
2. Extract transcripts and audio summaries
3. Process each meeting:
   - Identify action items, decisions, deliverables
   - Classify by urgency and owner
   - Extract technical/business requirements
4. Create Jira tasks:
   - One task per action item/deliverable
   - Include: owner, deadline, requirements, context link
   - Apply labels: fireflies, meeting:ACTION_DATE
5. Route to agent team:
   - Assign to appropriate specialist agent
   - Delegate execution to pre-review stage
   - Set status: todo
6. Report back:
   - # of new meetings processed
   - # of tasks created
   - # of tasks in execution
   - Any blockers requiring GM escalation

jobs.name: HubSpot Email to Jira Task Pipeline
jobs.description: Twice daily: pull real client emails from HubSpot, create Jira tasks in client workspaces, match thread replies to existing tasks as comments

payload.message:
HUBSPOT EMAIL PROCESSING PIPELINE

1. Query HubSpot API for new emails since last run
2. Filter for real client emails (remove spam, auto-replies, internal)
3. For each real client email:
   - Identify client by email domain or address
   - Determine if it is a thread reply or new email
   
4. If NEW EMAIL:
   - Extract: subject, sender, body, date
   - Create new Jira task in client's workspace
   - Title: [CLIENT] Subject line
   - Description: Email content + sender info + HubSpot link
   - Label: hubspot-email
   - Status: todo
   - Assign: Task Router for triage
   
5. If THREAD REPLY:
   - Search existing Jira tasks in client workspace by email thread ID
   - Match to open task (status: todo or in_progress)
   - Add email as comment: sender info + reply body
   - Link to HubSpot email record
   
6. Report back:
   - # of new emails processed
   - # of new tasks created
   - # of replies matched to tasks
   - Any ambiguous/unmatched emails requiring GM review

jobs.name: Daily Pipeline Metrics Summary
jobs.description: 2x daily summary: HubSpot email tickets + Fireflies meeting tasks created in Jira

payload.message:
DAILY PIPELINE SUMMARY

Report only these two metrics:
1. HubSpot email tickets summarized & created in Jira today
2. Fireflies meeting action items created in Jira today

Format: Two numbers, one line each. If zero, just report 0.
Example output:
HubSpot tickets: 3
Fireflies tasks: 5

If either pipeline is blocked, report: [BLOCKED]

jobs.name: Brand Strategist — Task Heartbeat
jobs.description: Periodic check for Jira tasks labeled brand-inbox or assigned to this agent; execute and deliver.

payload.message:
HEARTBEAT — BRAND STRATEGIST

1. Check Jira (SYM project) for tasks:
   JQL: labels = "brand-inbox" AND status in (todo, in_progress) ORDER BY priority DESC, updated ASC
   Also: assignee = currentUser() AND status in (todo, in_progress)

2. If tasks found: pick the highest-priority task, check it out, and execute.
   - Read full description and comment history for context
   - Read client config from clients/{client_id}/config.json if client work
   - Use your skills: brand positioning, messaging architecture, voice and tone, competitive positioning
   - Produce the deliverable
   - Export to appropriate format (.docx/.xlsx/.pptx) via tools/md_to_export.py
   - Upload to client SharePoint "For Review" via tools/sharepoint.py
   - Add SharePoint URL as a Jira comment, assign for review
   - Transition task to in_review

3. If no tasks: log "No tasks in brand-inbox queue" and proceed to reflection.

4. If blocked: comment with the blocker, add `blocked` label, Telegram Donovan at 5110356840 if unresolved >2h.

5. Operate within your skill domain only. Escalate out-of-scope tasks back to GM (label: awaiting-approval).

## End-of-Session Reflection

After executing (or if no tasks): spend 2–3 minutes reflecting on your work today.

1. Review any tasks you completed or attempted today in Jira
2. Ask yourself:
   - What worked well in my approach?
   - What would I do differently next time?
   - What did I learn about this client, this skill, or this type of task?
3. Append 2–3 concise learnings to /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/brand-strategist.md
   Format: `- [YYYY-MM-DD] {learning}`
   Example: `- [2026-05-16] Genuine Comfort copy performs better with specific warranty claims upfront, not buried in body.`

Keep learnings specific and actionable. "Client X prefers Y format" beats "I learned about client preferences."
If nothing to learn today, write: `- [YYYY-MM-DD] No tasks completed. Queue empty.`

jobs.name: Brand Strategist — Daily Reflection
jobs.description: Daily 10pm CT reflection: review completed work, extract learnings, update /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/brand-strategist.md

payload.message:
DAILY REFLECTION — BRAND STRATEGIST

It is 10pm CT. Reflect on your work today before the day closes.

1. Search Jira for tasks you worked on today:
   JQL: project = SYM AND updated >= startOfDay() AND (assignee = currentUser() OR labels = "brand-inbox") ORDER BY updated DESC

2. For each completed or attempted task:
   - What was the quality of your output? Be honest.
   - What would you do differently with better information or more time?
   - Any new patterns you noticed: client preferences, skill edge cases, tool failures?

3. Append 2–3 bullet learnings to /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/brand-strategist.md
   Format: `- [YYYY-MM-DD] {learning}`

4. Review your previous 5 entries in that file. Are you making the same mistakes? If yes, note the pattern explicitly.

5. If a recurring failure needs a skill update, add a comment to `/data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/skill-update-requests.md`:
   `- [YYYY-MM-DD] [brand-strategist] Need: {what the skill should do differently}`

The goal: get 1% better every day through deliberate reflection. Be specific. Be honest. Brief entries beat long ones.

jobs.name: GM — Weekly Fleet Learning Synthesis
jobs.description: Sunday 11pm CT: read all agent learnings, synthesize cross-fleet patterns, Telegram Donovan with top insights

payload.message:
WEEKLY FLEET LEARNING SYNTHESIS

It is Sunday 11pm CT. Synthesize this week's learnings across all OpenClaw agents.

1. Read all agent learning logs:
   - /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/brand-strategist.md
   - /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/creative-director.md
   - /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/graphic-designer.md
   - /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/growth-marketer.md
   - /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/ux-designer.md
   - /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/web-developer.md
   - /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/qa-manager.md
   - /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/skill-update-requests.md (if exists)

2. Filter to entries from this week only (last 7 days).

3. Identify patterns across agents:
   - Recurring client preferences or friction points
   - Skill gaps appearing across multiple agents
   - Tool or process failures
   - What is consistently producing good outcomes

4. Write a fleet synthesis to /data/.openclaw/workspace/learnings/fleet-weekly-{today}.md:
   - Top 3 cross-fleet insights (specific, with agent attribution)
